title: ricinus communis var. armatus
intro: Ricinus communis var. armatus, also known as the sharp-leaved castor oil plant, is a variant of the Ricinus communis plant that is characterized by its sharp, thorny leaves and stems. This plant is a rich source of ricinoleic acid, which is a potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compound with medicinal properties. The ricinoleic acid found in Ricinus communis var. armatus has been used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can provide relief from pain, reduce swelling, and promote healing. The herbal preparations of Ricinus communis var. armatus include oil and ointments, which can be used topically to alleviate skin conditions and promote wound healing.
